questions.questionText: The mechanism of DNA replication is described as:
questions.explanation: Each new DNA double helix contains one parental strand and one newly synthesized strand.

questions.questionText: DNA polymerase synthesizes DNA in which direction?
questions.optionD: It depends on the leading or lagging strand
questions.explanation: Nucleotides are always added to the 3' hydroxyl end of the growing chain.

questions.questionText: Okazaki fragments are formed during the synthesis of the:
questions.explanation: The lagging strand is synthesized discontinuously as Okazaki fragments.

questions.questionText: Which enzyme is responsible for removing the RNA primer and replacing it with DNA in prokaryotes?
questions.explanation: DNA Pol I possesses 5' to 3' exonuclease activity to remove primers.

questions.questionText: The promoter region in eukaryotes typically contains which consensus sequence?
questions.explanation: The TATA box (Hogness box) is found ~25 base pairs upstream of the transcription start site.

questions.questionText: Post-transcriptional modifications of eukaryotic mRNA include all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Exons are retained and spliced together to form the mature mRNA; introns are removed.

questions.questionText: The Shine-Dalgarno sequence in prokaryotic mRNA functions to:
questions.optionB: Bind to the 16S rRNA of the small ribosomal subunit
questions.explanation: It aligns the ribosome correctly so that translation begins at the AUG start codon.

questions.questionText: Which site on the ribosome accepts the incoming aminoacyl-tRNA?
questions.explanation: The Acceptor (A) site binds the incoming loaded tRNA.

questions.questionText: Restriction endonucleases typically recognize and cut DNA at:
questions.explanation: Type II restriction enzymes recognize specific short palindromic sequences (e.g., GAATTC).

questions.questionText: Polymerase Chain Reaction (PCR) involves three step cycles. What is the correct order?
questions.explanation: Heat denatures DNA, cool down to anneal primers, then synthesize (extend) DNA.

questions.questionText: In Molecular Biology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Biochemistry texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Biochemistry references for Molecular Biology. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Molecular Biology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Molecular Biology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Molecular Biology.
